mechanism_narrative: With FEDFUNDS pinned near zero (0.2%) in the aftermath of the financial crisis, the near-zero policy rate compresses the equity risk premium discount factor, mechanically supporting forward S&P 500 returns as investors seek yield in risk assets over cash and short bonds.
failure_condition: If SPX forward 20-day returns during periods of FEDFUNDS < 0.5% are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 2-year windows, reject hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in UNRATE of more than 0.2 percentage points over a 3-month window signals turning-point labor market healing, which historically precedes sustained equity rallies as forward earnings expectations improve over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls by more than 0.2pp over trailing 3 months
failure_condition: If triggered instances show negative or statistically insignificant 60-day forward returns (p > 0.05) in more than 40% of occurrences, or Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 2-year windows, reject hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in RSAFS (retail sales) and HOUST (housing starts) reflects broad-based consumer and residential investment demand recovery; when both series show simultaneous positive momentum, the combined signal is a stronger predictor of forward equity gains than either series alone because it confirms demand-side breadth rather than a single-sector rebound.
failure_condition: If the joint-signal model's Rank IC is not materially higher than the best single-series model (delta IC < 0.02) over any 2-year rolling window, or forward returns fail to differ significantly from unconditional baseline, reject hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: During the early post-recession recovery regime, positive month-over-month INDPRO growth signals restocking and capacity utilization improvement that feeds into corporate earnings over a quarterly horizon, driving positive forward S&P 500 returns; however this relationship is expected to weaken or reverse in late-cycle regimes where industrial expansion signals overheating and tightening risk.
failure_condition: If positive INDPRO growth periods do not produce significantly positive 60-day forward returns (p > 0.05) specifically in early-recovery regimes, or if the sign flips consistently in this regime rather than late-cycle, reject hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month positive change in PAYEMS (nonfarm payrolls) reflects real-time labor demand expansion; even small positive prints act as a leading indicator of near-term risk-on sentiment, producing modestly positive S&P 500 returns over the following week as markets price in improving income and consumption trajectories.
failure_condition: If 5-day forward returns following positive PAYEMS prints are not significantly different from returns following negative prints (p > 0.05) over any 2-year rolling window, reject hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year CPIAUCSL inflation crosses above 2.0%, markets begin pricing in expectations of Federal Reserve policy normalization, raising the anticipated discount rate path and compressing equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
failure_condition: If SPX 20-day forward returns following CPI crossings above 2.0% YoY are not significantly negative (p > 0.05), or the trigger fires with fewer than 5 instances in the historical sample making the test underpowered, reject hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in quarterly GDPC1 growth and monthly RSAFS growth indicates a synchronized, durable expansion in aggregate demand rather than a transitory bounce; this joint confirmation signal predicts sustained positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon more reliably than either indicator alone, since GDP growth without retail confirmation (or vice versa) often reflects noisy or inventory-driven data.
failure_condition: If the joint GDPC1-RSAFS signal's predictive power (Rank IC) does not exceed 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows, or is not statistically distinguishable from the single-series RSAFS model, reject hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: INDPRO growth above 2% annualized (3-month) combined with FEDFUNDS remaining below 0.5% signals a supply-side recovery unconstrained by monetary tightening, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month; this joint condition is regime-specific to the zero-rate recovery period and is expected to fail once the Fed begins raising rates even amid strong industrial growth.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month annualized growth exceeds 2% AND FEDFUNDS below 0.5%
failure_condition: If the joint trigger produces forward 20-day returns not significantly different from the unconditional baseline (p > 0.05), or if the relationship persists unchanged after FEDFUNDS rises above 0.5% (indicating the interaction term is not regime-dependent), reject hypothesis.
